Name: neural_network_compression
Title: Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Compression
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the compression of neural networks can be fundamentally limited by the information-theoretic tradeoff between the number of bits used to represent the network and the resulting distortion in its predictive performance. We aim to establish a rate-distortion theory for neural network compression, providing a framework for understanding the limits of compression and guiding the development of more efficient compression methods.
Related Work: While previous work has explored various techniques for compressing neural networks, such as pruning and quantization, a comprehensive understanding of the fundamental limits of compression is lacking. Our proposal builds on recent advances in information-theoretic approaches to machine learning, applying rate-distortion theory to the specific problem of neural network compression.
Abstract: This proposal explores the information-theoretic limits of neural network compression, seeking to establish a rate-distortion theory that characterizes the fundamental tradeoff between compression and predictive performance. We will develop new distortion measures and achievability bounds for neural network compression, providing a framework for understanding the limits of compression and guiding the development of more efficient methods. Our work will contribute to the advancement of neural network compression, enabling the deployment of more efficient and effective models in resource-constrained environments.
Experiments: Develop and evaluate new distortion measures for neural network compression, capturing the impact of compression on predictive performance
Establish achievability bounds for neural network compression, characterizing the minimum number of bits required to achieve a given level of predictive performance
Evaluate the performance of existing compression methods in relation to the established rate-distortion bounds, identifying areas for improvement
Risk Factors and Limitations: The development of meaningful distortion measures for neural network compression may be challenging, requiring careful consideration of the relationship between compression and predictive performance
The establishment of tight achievability bounds may require significant mathematical advances, potentially limiting the scope of the results
